From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Nora Mead Brownell](/w/index.php?title=Nora_Mead_Brownell&redirect=no "Nora Mead Brownell"))

American businesswoman

| Nora Brownell | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | [Erie, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Erie,_Pennsylvania "Erie, Pennsylvania") |
| Education | [Syracuse University](/wiki/Syracuse_University "Syracuse University") |
| Known for | Former Commissioner for the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission |

**Nora Mead Brownell** (born May 1947) is the co-founder of Espy Energy Solutions, LLC, an energy consulting firm and a former Commissioner of the [Federal Energy Regulatory Commission](/wiki/Federal_Energy_Regulatory_Commission "Federal Energy Regulatory Commission") (FERC) from 2001–2006 under the administration of President [George W. Bush](/wiki/George_W._Bush "George W. Bush"). She is a former President of the National Association of Regulatory Utility Commissioners (NARUC).

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nora_Brownell&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Brownell is a native of [Erie, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Erie,_Pennsylvania "Erie, Pennsylvania") and attended [Syracuse University](/wiki/Syracuse_University "Syracuse University").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nora_Brownell&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

In 1987, Brownell served as the Deputy Executive Assistant to Pennsylvania Governor [Richard Thornburgh](/wiki/Richard_Thornburgh "Richard Thornburgh"). Her career in energy began when she served as a Commissioner of the Pennsylvania Public Utility Commission (PUC) from 1997 to 2001.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On April 30, 2001 President George W. Bush nominated Brownell to serve as a [Commissioner for the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission](/wiki/Federal_Energy_Regulatory_Commission "Federal Energy Regulatory Commission").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) Her nomination was confirmed by the [United States Senate](/wiki/United_States_Senate "United States Senate") a month later on May 25. She served until July 21, 2006.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Following the conclusion of her term as a Commissioner with FERC, Brownell helped found the energy consulting firm, ESPY Energy Solutions, based in [Alexandria, Virginia](/wiki/Alexandria,_Virginia "Alexandria, Virginia").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

She served as the Board Chair for [Pacific Gas and Electric](/wiki/Pacific_Gas_and_Electric "Pacific Gas and Electric").[[6]](#cite_note-6) She has been a Board Member for many corporations including [National Grid](/wiki/National_Grid_plc "National Grid plc"), [Spectra Energy](/wiki/Spectra_Energy "Spectra Energy"), [Oncor Electric Delivery](/wiki/Oncor_Electric_Delivery "Oncor Electric Delivery"), and [Times Publishing Company](/wiki/Times_Publishing_Company "Times Publishing Company").[[7]](#cite_note-7) In addition, she has lectured at the [University of Vermont](/wiki/University_of_Vermont "University of Vermont") Law School, [Michigan State University](/wiki/Michigan_State_University "Michigan State University"), and the [University of Idaho](/wiki/University_of_Idaho "University of Idaho").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
